meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build email automation and overcome a lack of portfolio experience. An intermediate-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Transform your career by building an automated email marketing engine. This project demonstrates high-demand skills in API integration, database management, and workflow automation. By shipping a functional, revenue-generating tool, you move from a student with no experience to a developer with a tangible product and verified technical expertise.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the cursor blinking in an empty IDE—the silent, crushing weight of a portfolio with nothing to show. You have the theoretical knowledge, but the industry demands proof. You are stuck in the 'junior gap,' where every job description feels like a fortress wall you cannot climb.

But the wall is an illusion. The most powerful way to break through is to stop applying for jobs and start building systems. By constructing an autonomous email automation platform, you move beyond 'todo apps' and into the realm of real-world infrastructure. This is how you prove your worth: not by listing skills, but by demonstrating a live, automated machine that solves a business problem.

steps.title: Architecting the Data Flow
steps.description: Define the schema for your email subscriber database and the trigger events for automated sequences.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a PostgreSQL schema for an email automation platform including tables for users, campaigns, and subscriber engagement metrics. Include indexing strategies for high-volume reads.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'create db schema and migrations for email automation platform'

steps.title: Integrating Email Delivery APIs
steps.description: Securely connect to Resend or SendGrid to handle transactional and marketing emails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular service in Node.js to interface with the Resend API, including error handling, rate limiting, and batch sending capabilities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement email service module with error handling and rate limiting'

steps.title: Building the Automation Engine
steps.description: Construct the CRON job logic to trigger emails based on user behavior or time-based intervals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automation engine that processes queue tasks from a Redis store and triggers specific email sequences based on user activity timestamps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'build Redis-backed queue processor for automated email triggers'

steps.title: Implementing Template Management
steps.description: Build a system to dynamic inject user data into HTML email templates using Handlebars.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a template engine that parses JSON user data into HTML email templates, ensuring responsive design and fallback values for missing fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement dynamic HTML template parser with Handlebars'

steps.title: Deployment and Observability
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel/Render and set up health checks to ensure your automation never goes down.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a production-ready CI/CD pipeline and add logging for email delivery success/failure rates to a dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup CI/CD pipeline and logging for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always implement exponential backoff on your API calls to ensure you don't get banned by email providers during traffic spikes.
Use a 'hard-bounce' monitoring system to automatically clean your subscriber list; high bounce rates will destroy your domain reputation.
Track open rates and clicks by injecting invisible tracking pixels into your HTML templates, not just via API webhooks.
Store your email content in a CMS or database rather than hardcoding it, allowing non-technical users to edit copy without a deployment.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your staging environment to test sequences with dummy data before triggering real emails to production lists.
content.next_steps: Once your platform is live, add a 'Case Study' page to your portfolio. Include the architecture diagram, the specific business problem you solved, and the metrics you tracked. Share this on LinkedIn to demonstrate your ability to build production-grade infrastructure.
content.conversion_block: You now have the blueprint to turn a blank screen into a professional-grade automated system. This is the difference between a student and a hired engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
